data.title: JBG SMITH, Cousins Properties downgraded at BMO Capital
data.description:
JBG SMITH Properties (NYSE:JBGS) and Cousins Properties (NYSE:CUZ) were trading lower Tuesday pre-market after BMO Capital downgraded the office REITs.

JBG SMITH's near-term office expirations remain elevated, while one of the weakest office markets in the country, Washington, DC, faces further uncertainty on the "DOGE Plan to Reform Government".

"Amazon's HQ2 has so far not able to be a giant tech magnet for National Landing, leading to declining office occupancy, reduced margins and earnings for JBGS. DOGE has an unknown and potentially meaningful impact on office and multifamily demand in DC metro, where JBGS is solely focused," said analyst John Kim in a research note.

The investment bank lowered its recommendation on the REIT to Underperform from Market Perform, and reduced the price target to $15 from $18. JBGS was trading 1.67% lower at $16.47.

Meanwhile, Cousins Properties was downgraded on its strong performance year-to-date. CUZ shares have risen 28.54% year-to-date, but were 0.48% down Tuesday pre-market to $31.15.

"CUZ has benefited from a flight-to-quality to its lifestyle office portfolio, but we have less conviction of its market fundamentals," said John Kim in a separate report.

"The office sector has been a relative winner YTD and is a beneficiary from the recent elections, but we see the sector performing in line with REITs in 2025," said BMO Capital Markets in its 2025 outlook for office REITs.

BXP (BXP), Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT), SL Green Realty (SLG), and Vornado Realty Trust (VNO) are BMO's top picks among office REITs.

data.title: Kane Biotech announces $3M private placement offering
data.description:
• Kane Biotech (TSXV:KNE:CA) announced on Tuesday that it intends to undertake a non-brokered private placement offering of up to 30M common shares at a price of $0.10 per share for proceeds of up to $3M.
  • The proceeds from the offering will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Hyundai Motor America November 2024 sales up 8% year-over-year
data.description:
• Hyundai Motor America (OTCPK:HYMTF) reported on Tuesday, a record-breaking total November sales of 76,008 units, an 8% increase compared with November 2023.
  • Retail sales for November 2024 set new records, climbing 15%. EV retail sales increased 77% year-over-year, hybrid retail sales grew by 104% and electrified retail sales jumped 92%.

data.title: Western Digital in focus as Citi trims price target but stays positive into spin-off
data.description:
• Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) was in focus on Tuesday as Citi trimmed its price target on the memory maker but reiterated its Buy rating ahead of the company's split of its flash memory and hard disk drive businesses, slated to occur sometime before the end of the year.
  • Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading.
  • "While we recognize NAND near-term fundamentals remain volatile, we continue to remain constructive on the forthcoming spin-off unlocking value," Citi analyst Asiya Merchant wrote in a note to clients.
  • Merchant, who lowered her price target to $85 from $87, added that at current levels, the hard disk drive accounts for roughly 95% of Western Digital's valuation, while the flash business is roughly 5%.

data.title: UBS favors buy-rated DECK, WWW, TJX, and ONON
data.description:
The UBS global research team came out on Tuesday and showcased that when scanning through its quantitative factor analysis four names popped up as buy-ratings. Those four names were Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK), Wolverine World Wide (NYSE:WWW), TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), and On Holding AG (NYSE:ONON).

UBS added that it believes DECK, WWW, TJX, and ONON each have solid fundamental outlooks. See below what the financial institution had to say about each of the four stocks:

DECK: “Hoka is one of the world's fastest growing footwear brands. We forecast it delivering a 21% 5-yr. sales CAGR and this is the main driver of our 15.5% 5-yr. EPS CAGR forecast for DECK. We believe this growth should drive stock outperformance.”

WWW: “WWW has streamlined its portfolio by divesting and licensing brands over the last 2 years, allowing it to re-invest in its business. We think its Active segment's return to growth and cost savings initiatives should drive a 25% 4-yr. EPS CAGR post FY24E.”

TJX: “We view TJX as capable of taking major market share from Department Store peers over the next few years. Plus, we believe TJX's newer businesses such as HomeSense and Sierra Trading Post have big potential, as does its international operation. We forecast TJX delivering a ~12.5% 5-yr. EPS CAGR.”

ONON: “We view On as one of the world's fastest growing athletic wear brands and worthy of a premium multiple. We forecast On delivering a 43% 5-year EPS CAGR and think its growth will surprise the market, driving stock outperformance.”

Year-to-date price action: DECK +81.2%, WWW +164.6%, TJX +34.5%, and ONON +117%.

data.title: Nerdy regains compliance with NYSE
data.description:
• Nerdy (NYSE:NRDY) said it has regained compliance with the New York Stock Exchange.
  • The company was not in compliance with the minimum share price requirement.

data.title: Scotiabank Q4 earnings top consensus, but revenue falls short
data.description:
The Bank of Nova Scotia (NYSE:BNS), known familiarly as Scotiabank, turned in better-than-expected earnings on Tuesday as its noninterest expenses dropped from a year ago, but revenue missed the mark, both in net interest income and noninterest income.

Fiscal Q4 EPS of C$1.57 (US$), topping the Visible Alpha consensus of C$1.60, fell from C$1.63 in Q3 and rose from C$1.23 in Q4 2023.

Net interest income for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024, increased to C$4.92B from C$4.86B in the previous quarter and C$4.67B in the year-ago period, but missed the Visible Alpha consensus of C$4.95B.

Noninterest income of C$3.60B, lagging the Visible Alpha estimate of C$3.69B, climbed from C$3.50B in Q3 and edged down from C$3.61B in last year's Q4.

Scotiabank's (NYSE:BNS) total revenue of C$8.53B grew from C$8.36B in the prior quarter and C$8.27B a year ago. The current quarter's revenue missed the Visible Alpha estimate of C$8.64B.

Scotiabank (BNS) ordinary shares fell 2.0% in premarket trading.

its provision for credit losses declined to C$1.03B from C$1.05B in Q3 and C$1.26B in Q4 2023.

Noninterest expenses of C$5.30B increased from C$4.95B in the previous quarter and dropped from C$5.53B a year ago.

Common equity tier 1 capital ratio of 13.1% slipped from 13.3% in Q3 and edged up from 13.0% in last year's Q4.

Average total net loans and acceptances of C$756B fell from C$761B in Q3. Average deposits of C$712B compared with C$711B in the prior quarter.

Canadian Banking adjusted net income of C$1.06B fell 4% Q/Q and climbed 34% Y/Y.

Global Wealth Management adjusted net income of C$426M rose 3% Q/Q and 28% Y/Y. Revenue climbed 13% Y/Y on higher mutual fund fees and brokerage revenue across Canadian and international wealth businesses.

Global Banking and Markets net income of C$403M dropped 4% Q/Q and 3% Y/Y. Business Banking revenue declined 4% Y/Y and Capital Markets revenue increased 7% Y/Y.

International Banking adjusted net income of C$634M dipped 4% Q/Q and increased 18% Y/Y.

Earlier, Bank of Nova Scotia non-GAAP EPS of C$1.57, revenue of C$8.53B

data.title: BlackRock AAA CLO ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.2605
data.description:
• BlackRock AAA CLO ETF (NASDAQ:CLOA) - $0.2605.
  • Payable Dec 05; for shareholders of record Dec 02; ex-div Dec 02.

data.title: AURI to launch gold backed crypto coin
data.description:
• Auri (OTCPK:AURI), on Tuesday, announced a joint venture with Gold Diamond Wawa Property Trust for acquisition/development of six patented claims in McMurray Township, in Canada.
  • The properties are located near the Red Pine's Wawa gold project, where recent drilling results indicate resource estimates north of 3 million ounces of gold.
  • Auri will own and collateralize the Gold reserves, and use the added value for its AU, Auri Tokens, as a "Gold Backed" crypto coin. Auri crypto coins are listed on the Salavi global exchange.

data.title: Cunard cruise bookings up 23% from last year
data.description:
With the launch of its newest ship, the Queen Anne, and three new cruise itineraries, Cunard (NYSE:CCL) saw the highest number of bookings for the first 10 months of 2024, a 23% increase from the same period last year.

Between January 1 and October 31, the British cruise line saw a 49% increase in first-time guests, with more than half of its total bookings now coming from outside the UK.

“As we look ahead to 2025, we are proud to report record-breaking bookings that demonstrate the continued strength of Cunard's commercial performance,” said Cunard President, Katie McAlister.

Carnival (CCL) purchased a 68% stake in Cunard in 1998 and the remaining 32% a year later.
